name: Chippewa Indian Fried Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine flour, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl.
2. In a separate bowl, mix warm water, vegetable oil, and nonfat dry milk powder until the mixture is smooth. Stir this into the flour mixture until a smooth dough forms.
3. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Knead it 4 times to form a smooth ball. Cover the dough and let it rest for 10 minutes.
4. Divide the dough into 8 balls. Flatten each with your fingertips or roll them out to form 8- to 10-inch rounds.
5. Make a small hole in the center of each round using your finger or the handle of a wooden spoon.
6. Lightly flour the rounds, stack them, and cover with a towel or plastic wrap.
7. Heat about 1 inch of vegetable oil to 375°F (190°C) in a large skillet.
8. Gently place one bread round into the hot fat and cook until golden and crisp, about 1 to 2 minutes on each side. Drain on paper towels.
9. Repeat with the remaining dough.
10. Serve the bread hot or at room temperature, sprinkled with cinnamon sugar.
